findings.issue: User-spec still leaks tech-spec-level details into AC and 'Технічні рішення' sections after round-1 cleanup. AC line 63 names the exact package and version `@nuxtjs/i18n@^9`, the exact config file `nuxt.config.ts`, the exact strategy enum value `prefix_except_default`, and the exact cookie attribute values `Secure on HTTPS, SameSite Lax`. AC line 76 names the exact API `useLocaleHead()` in the exact file `app.vue`. AC line 79 names the exact E2E spec path `e2e/i18n.spec.ts`. The verification table at lines 144-151 hardcodes paths like `frontend/i18n/locales/en.json` and `e2e/i18n.spec.ts`. The 'Технічні рішення' section names APIs (`useLocaleHead`, `getRouteBaseName`, `redirectOn: 'no prefix'`), JSON structure conventions ('nested JSON, camelCase ключі, namespaces по фічах'), and bundling strategy ('Eager-load обох локалей'). The 'Ризики' section names `getRouteBaseName` and prescribes `computed()` wrappers for Zod schemas.
findings.why_matters: User-spec defines WHAT and WHY (observable behavior, business rationale); tech-spec defines HOW (file layout, API names, config keys, structure conventions). Naming the package version, strategy enum, exact API names, and JSON structure in AC ties the implementation to a single concrete approach and prevents the tech-spec author from proposing alternatives (e.g. a composable that hides `useLocaleHead`, or a different version pin if v9 turns out to be broken on Nuxt 4). Round-1 removed the worst leaks (numbered middleware filename, exact composable signature, raw call-site counts) but the residual leakage in AC and 'Технічні рішення' still encodes implementation choices.
findings.fix: Move package version (`^9`), strategy enum (`prefix_except_default`), specific API names (`useLocaleHead`, `getRouteBaseName`), JSON structure conventions (nested, camelCase, namespaces), bundling decisions (eager-load), Zod fix mechanics (`computed()` wrapper), and exact filesystem paths (`frontend/i18n/locales/en.json`, `e2e/i18n.spec.ts`, `app.vue`) from user-spec into tech-spec. Keep AC at observable-behavior level: 'cookie persists locale across visits with 1y TTL, Secure on HTTPS, no cross-site leak' instead of 'SameSite Lax'; '`<html lang>` updates on switch' instead of '`useLocaleHead()` in `app.vue`'; 'one Playwright E2E covering switch flow' without naming the file path. The verification table can keep the bash commands but should be in the tech-spec / AVP, not user-spec.

summary: Round-1 fixes correctly removed the optional CI grep gate from AC (now explicitly out of scope in 'Обмеження') and added rationale for the invalid-prefix-redirect over 404 (UX preference documented). Sizing finding from round 1 was already non-actionable for user-spec. Residual major issue: AC and 'Технічні рішення' still encode HOW-level details (package version, strategy enum, exact API names, JSON structure conventions, exact file paths in verification table). No critical findings — approved with one major issue to address during tech-spec authoring.
